Act, 2016. Therefore, we direct the authorities concerned to 
declare separate cut-off marks and publish separate merit list 
for the PwD category at every stage of the examination and 
proceed with the selection process accordingly.
64. At this juncture, this Court reiterates that for the purpose of 
rights and entitlements of persons with disabilities, particularly in 
employment, and more specifically in respect of the issues covered 
in this judgment, there can be no distinction between Persons with 
[2025] 4 S.C.R.  
319
In Re: Recruitment of Visually Impaired in Judicial Services
Disabilities (PwD) and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). 
It is made abundantly clear that any such technical distinction sought 
to be made by the authorities cannot be sustained in law. It will be 
appropriate to state that this principle has been maintained by this 
Court and the same is evident from the observation made in Vikash 
Kumar as follows:
